story_angle: investigative exposé uncovering a hidden scandal
headline: The Unraveling of the Imperial Energy Consortium: A Call to Arms
deck: In a tapestry woven with threads of greed and misconduct, the noble pillars of our empire tremble under the weight of corruption.
article:
In the year of our Lord nineteen hundred and fifty-four, the revered halls of the Imperial Energy Consortium, once a beacon of Roman ingenuity and progress, are now engulfed in scandal. A covert partnership has emerged between the Consortium and foreign corporations, jeopardizing not only the very sovereignty of Rome but also the welfare of its citizens. As the sun sets on our esteemed empire, shadows of betrayal gather thickly like fog over the Tiber.

Recent testimonies from brave whistleblowers within the Consortium have revealed a grotesque misappropriation of the empire's energy resources. This betrayal, orchestrated by high-ranking officials who have long prioritized greed over virtue, has sparked a disquieting wave of environmental degradation and public health crises, casting a pall over the lives of innocent Romans. Pollution now chokes the air once filled with the sweet scent of olive blossoms, and passionate citizens are left to contend with ailments born from the negligence of those sworn to protect them.

At the heart of this insidious web of corruption, figures such as Lucius Aemilius, the Consortium's chief financial steward, and his entourage of sycophants are implicated, their coffers swelling at the expense of the common good. “We have sacrificed our very essence for fleeting profits,” lamented Marcella Cato, a whistleblower and former operations officer, in a clandestine meeting. “The empire's prosperity must not come at the cost of our health and dignity.” Her words resonate with urgency, echoing through the once-gleaming corridors now tarnished by deceit.

As the Roman populace, ignited by righteous indignation, rises in solidarity, it becomes clear that the moment for action is upon us. The imperial decree issued in response to these revelations is but a feeble attempt to placate the anger of the masses. Citizens, fervent in their quest for accountability, demand sweeping reforms to dismantle this corrupt nexus of power. A reckoning looms on the horizon, challenging the imperial authorities to choose between the relentless pursuit of profit and the sacred duty to uphold the welfare of the empire's beloved citizens. Thus, dear readers, let us rally forth and restore the integrity of Rome, a jewel of civilization, tarnished yet resolutely striving toward redemption.
author: Foreign Bureau

oped.title: Let the Consortium Burn
oped.author: Cassius Longinus
oped.body: The Empire's atomic monopoly unravels and the Senate demands blood. Yet this isn't collapse—it's overdue reckoning. For generations we've worshipped fission like false gods, chaining provinces to radioactive quotas while patricians toast in lead-lined villas. A call to arms? Spare us. Let the reactors crack, the togas char. True Romans once ruled with aqueducts and virtue, not fallout and fear. If eternity demands this poison, let Rome finally die.

ads.text: Experience the future of luxury living! Be the first to secure your atomically-powered villa in the heart of Neo-Rome. Equipped with radiant heating and the latest in plutonium appliances. Only available to discerning citizens. Call today to schedule your exclusive tour!

Join the sensational 'Atomic Spectacle' at the Colosseum! An electrifying show featuring the most daring acrobats performing under the glow of atomic lights. Show starts at dusk every Saturday. Tickets available at all Vespasian Kiosks!

Help shape the future! The Imperial Atomic Energy Commission is seeking skilled workers for our new power plant. Competitive salaries and bonuses for those who bring innovation to our atomic efforts. Join us in leading Rome into the atomic age!

Discover the wonders of our atomic world! Book your tickets for a luxury cruise to the Mediterranean aboard the SS Plutonium. State-of-the-art amenities powered by the latest atomic technology! Reserve your cabin now—spaces are limited!

panels.caption: Senator, the Imperial Energy Consortium's atomic vaults list 40 tons of uranium as 'gifts to the legions'.
The Praetorian Prefect nods: 'Correct. Each cohort deserves its own private pile for morale during parades.'
The lead engineer shrugs: 'Then the vaporized quarter of Rome is merely our new public sauna.'

joke.setup: With the Imperial Energy Consortium unraveling faster than a reactor core in a Gaulish winter, the Senate issued its atomic call to arms.
joke.punchline: Turns out the only thing the Empire can still enrich is the uranium already inside the Praetorian Guard.
